wildcard_get_fp_quantification_per_a2_am.context: Premortem dab0766 R8 / T2/E1 surfaced wildcard `("*", "get")` at RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns becoming LIVE post-M2. M3 quantifies FP rate on synthetic Rust .get(...) patterns.

fixtures.source_summary: let key = std::env::var("KEY").unwrap(); let map: HashMap<String,i32> = HashMap::new(); let _v = map.get(&key);
fixtures.verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — HashMap::get is not an HTTP request. Wildcard pattern fires on .get(<tainted>) call regardless of receiver type. Premortem R8 confirmed.
fixtures.ssrf_findings_per_call: 3 (likely one per source-edge into the .get call from var()/unwrap()/key — multi-source amplification at canonical layer)

fixtures.source_summary: let idx_str = std::env::var("IDX").unwrap(); let idx: usize = idx_str.parse().unwrap(); let v: Vec<i32> = vec![1,2,3]; let _x = v.get(idx);
fixtures.verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — Vec::get is not an HTTP request. Same wildcard FP class.

fixtures.source_summary: let k = std::env::var("K").unwrap(); let m: BTreeMap<String,i32> = BTreeMap::new(); let _v = m.get(&k);
fixtures.verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — BTreeMap::get is not an HTTP request. Same wildcard FP class.
TRUE POSITIVE — closes rust_ssrf_positive bank gap; SSRF flow correctly identified. New ('','reqwest::blocking::get') member_pattern is the discriminating sink.
TRUE POSITIVE — FileOpen path. Multi-source amplification (5 findings on same line).

fp_summary.interpretation: On the universe of `<receiver>.get(<tainted>)` Rust call expressions where receiver type is NOT a reqwest/ureq HTTP client, the wildcard fires 100% of the time as a FP. This is a pre-existing wildcard pattern not introduced by this milestone, but it becomes LIVE on .rs files for the first time post-M2 (vuln-migration-v1 routed .rs to line scanner only). Magnitude on real-world Rust codebases is unmeasured but expected to be HIGH (HashMap::get / Vec::get / Option::get are pervasive).
fp_summary.carry_forward_recommendation: Surface as a follow-on milestone `rust-wildcard-get-narrowing-v1`: tighten ('*', 'get') and ('*', 'post') to receiver-type-aware patterns (e.g., only fire when receiver type resolves to reqwest::Client / reqwest::blocking::Client / ureq::Agent / ureq::Request). Out of M2 atomic-commit boundary; M2 stop-thresholds (4 RED→GREEN, 0 GREEN→RED, FP regression-guards stay GREEN) are met regardless. Production user code MAY produce inflated SSRF finding counts on Rust files post-publish until narrowing milestone lands.

non_rust_dispatch_sanity_check.context: Verify M2 dispatch flip did not break Python/JS/etc. canonical pipeline on .rs alongside line scanner — non-Rust extensions still skip line scanner.
non_rust_dispatch_sanity_check.test: Pre-M2 binary on same Python XSS fixture vs post-M2 binary on same fixture — both produce 0 XSS findings. The XSS detection has a pre-existing gap unrelated to this milestone (verified via `git show HEAD:vuln.rs` rebuild + side-by-side smoke). Documented as a separate carry-forward.
non_rust_dispatch_sanity_check.post_m2_observation_python_xss: 0 XSS findings (matches HEAD baseline; pre-existing gap; NOT an M2 regression)
non_rust_dispatch_sanity_check.test_vuln_detects_xss_status: FAILED at HEAD (pre-existing, verified by HEAD-restore experiment); FAILED post-M2 (UNCHANGED). NOT counted as an M2-induced regression per stop-threshold #5 ('0 currently-GREEN tests transition RED'); the test was already RED at HEAD.

rule_id_distribution_for_rust_files.post_m2: Pre-M2 set + CWE-22 (PathTraversal), CWE-918 (Ssrf), CWE-502 (Deserialization), CWE-79 (Xss) when canonical pipeline detects taint flow
rule_id_distribution_for_rust_files.consumer_impact: Reframe C in vuln-migration-v1 §0 was internal; CHANGELOG flags the behavior change. Exhaustive map_core_vuln_type guards against silent variant relabel.
